---
title: "Advanced Empirical Finance"
subtitle: "Topics and Data Science"
toc: false
---

The guiding question of this course: **(How) can state-of-the-art methods improve financial decision-making?** The lecture covers the theory and the recent papers — but most of your effort should go into actually *doing* empirical work.

```{=html}
<div class="grid home-cards">
  <a class="home-card g-col-12 g-col-md-6 g-col-lg-4" href="exercises/technical-prerequisites.html">
    <span class="home-card-title">🚀 Start here</span>
    <span class="home-card-text">Get R, Python &amp; Positron ready before the first lecture (Exercise&nbsp;0).</span>
  </a>
  <a class="home-card g-col-12 g-col-md-6 g-col-lg-4" href="slides-index.html">
    <span class="home-card-title">🗓️ Lecture plan</span>
    <span class="home-card-text">Week-by-week schedule with readings and assignment deadlines.</span>
  </a>
  <a class="home-card g-col-12 g-col-md-6 g-col-lg-4" href="slides.html">
    <span class="home-card-title">📊 Slides</span>
    <span class="home-card-text">View and download every lecture deck, embedded on one page.</span>
  </a>
  <a class="home-card g-col-12 g-col-md-6 g-col-lg-4" href="exercises/index.html">
    <span class="home-card-title">✏️ Exercises</span>
    <span class="home-card-text">Hands-on problem sets that build the skills the lectures introduce.</span>
  </a>
  <a class="home-card g-col-12 g-col-md-6 g-col-lg-4" href="additional-material/index.html">
    <span class="home-card-title">📚 More resources</span>
    <span class="home-card-text">Guides on Quarto, Git, Positron AI, and machine-learning workflows.</span>
  </a>
</div>
```

## About the exercises

The [exercise sets](exercises/index.qmd) partially subsume work I created with my colleagues Christoph Scheuch and Patrick Weiss for the books [Tidy Finance with R](https://www.tidy-finance.org/r/index.html) and [Tidy Finance with Python](https://www.tidy-finance.org/python/index.html). Feedback on *every* aspect is very welcome — if you spot typos, mistakes, or anything that deserves more attention, contact me or my colleagues at [contact@tidy-finance.org](mailto:contact@tidy-finance.org).

Try to solve each question on your own before consulting the proposed answers, and discuss issues with your peers. The most direct way to get help is to post your questions on StackExchange or Absalon. As an absolute minimum, make sure you are familiar with Grolemund and Wickham's [R for Data Science](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/) before starting.
